Imbued Platinum Diamond Ring  
 


Slot: FINGER
STA: +5 WIS: +5
SV FIRE: +5 SV DISEASE: +5 SV COLD: +5 SV MAGIC: +5 SV POISON: +5
WT: 0.1 Size: TINY
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Deity: Mithaniel Marr
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Imbued Platinum Diamond Ring

Slot: FINGER
STA: +5 WIS: +5
SV FIRE: +5 SV DISEASE: +5 SV COLD: +5 SV MAGIC: +5 SV POISON: +5
WT: 0.1 Size: TINY
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Deity: Mithaniel Marr
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Lore:Mithaniel Platinum Diamond Ring
Item Type:Jewelry
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:315 pp 0 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:720
Submitted By:Skrimgeour Kuchenhyrst
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-07-27 07:12:32
Page Updated:Tue Apr 19th, 2022

Expansion: Original
Average Price: No Data Pricing Data...
Rarity: Common
Level to Attain: 1
[Comments ]

Crafted: This item is crafted by players.
  • Imbued Platinum Diamond Ring (Jewelcraft, 287 trivial)
    • Class: ALL
    Expansion: Original
    Yield: 1 x Imbued Platinum Diamond Ring
Progression server item.


Uploaded November 27th, 2008
Send a correction
Post Comment
Resist gear...not
QuoteReply
# May 13 2003 at 11:50 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592245745802
View User Forum
Sage
950 posts
The UN-imbued platium diamond ring adds 7 to all saves, but gives no stats. If you're wearing one of these for the resists, you're better off with the unimbued version. Curious...
▲ Page top
RE: Resist gear...not
QuoteReply
# Jul 12 2003 at 3:56 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
Well if resists are all you want, yes, the unimbued is slightly better. But for an extra 5 wis and sta, I think it's be worth it to sacrfice a mere 2 sv everything. Overall I'd still prefer the imubed version.
▲ Page top
I didn't know
QuoteReply
# Jan 15 2002 at 8:48 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592252775636
5 posts
I asked jewerler in Gfay to make this ring for me, but she failed first time. She tried to make again and made it.
I was happy and got this ring after paying her.
But I couldn't wear this ring because of DEITY.
Yes.. I am a daughter of Tunare. That made me crazy, I have been looked for cleric who could imbue diamond for a long time.
I should have known this.
All I can do is to sell this ring. stat is really great, I think this is best wis ring for Priest class.
If u want to get this ring, but u are not follower of M,R, You can get Diamond wedding band which is dropped at Shadowtower in Ice clad.
Nice stat too, but it is lore, so u can have only this ring.

I am gonna sell this ring for 1k, but is there someone to buy it?

60 Druid
Colorless Holyflora
on Tunare

▲ Page top
RE: I didn't know
QuoteReply
# Apr 18 2003 at 3:42 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
Um, you'd best know the gem of your deity, hehe. Tunare is emerald (stats on imbued emerald jewelery aren't really worth making them, in my opinion). All items made out of imbued diamonds are Mithaniel Marr only.

And you're wrong, this ring is NOT lore, you CAN wear two of them (assuming you worship Mithaniel Marr). Unless you were talking about the TOFS ring.

With the resists, stamina, and wisdom, this makes a great item for clerics and palladins of Mithaniel Marr. Shamans too, for you froggie shamans out there.

For more information on imbued jewelry, go here:
http://www.eqtraders.com/secrets/recipes_jewlcraft_imbued.htm

Edited, Fri Apr 18 04:08:57 2003
▲ Page top
price?
QuoteReply
# Sep 26 2001 at 10:15 PM Rating: Good
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
price?
▲ Page top
RE: price?
QuoteReply
# May 13 2003 at 5:27 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
Current market, diamonds sell for about 200pp. Platinum bars cost about 100pp. So I'd say 600 platinum (double cost of materials) if you supplied no ingredients. You supply all the ingredients yourself (including getting the diamond imbued) you could probably convince a jewelcrafter to do the combine for you for 100pp. Note that you'd need a 250 jewelcrafter to make this if you want a good chance at success.
▲ Page top
RE: price?
QuoteReply
# Oct 14 2001 at 8:38 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I was quoted 700p with me supplying the imbued diamond, not the most looked for piece out there, so best to find a diamond and get it imbued, then go shopping for a jeweler. Seems that any cleric with the imbue diamond spell can do it, so the spell itself is not diety specific ( had a follower of Brell imbue mine ).
▲ Page top
RE: price?
QuoteReply
# May 13 2003 at 5:09 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
Um, you did NOT have a follower of Brell imbue the diamond, that I can guarantee you, becuase only Mithaniel Marr clerics (and shamans) can cast imbue diamond. The spell IS deity specific, don't know where you got the idea wasn't (the PoP imbue spells for raw diamonds are not deity specific, but all imbue spells for old world gems ARE).

However the religion of the person doing the jewelcraft combine is irrelevant; any jcer can make it, but only Mithanier Marr worshippers can imbue the diamond or wear the finished product.

Edited, Tue May 13 05:36:34 2003
▲ Page top
Diety specific
QuoteReply
# Aug 30 2001 at 6:50 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591766115346
1 post
Diety specific jewelry is so lame and stupid of verant. That locks out 95 to 98% of the people that could really use this stuff. As a cleric, I need my wisdom to help keep my mana pool up but also need resist. Why should only one diety that has like a 3% following get it?

Diety specific armor is one thing but jewelry is whole different thing.
▲ Page top
RE: Diety specific
QuoteReply
# May 13 2003 at 5:14 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
You'll notice that the imbued jewelery has better stats that the equivalent non-imbued one. If this were made with a non-imbued diamond, it would be resists and nothing else; imbued, it also gives wis and sta.

Just like the armor, imbuing it gives better stats than it would have otherwise, to reward the followers of specific deities.

If it weren't for deity specific items, most people (who had that option for their race/class) would be agnostic for faction reasons. It's not stupid or lame.
▲ Page top
RE: Diety specific
QuoteReply
# Sep 20 2001 at 3:43 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592791169316
200 posts
verant made diety jewelry for a reason lenaras you may think its stupid to have it but then what would be the purpose of having dieties

seems your just jelous because you chose agnostic over mith mar im just POed that i cant seem to locate innorruuk jewelry for my SK and necro my wizard has some hella nice rings for solro wizards
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# Jun 07 2001 at 1:02 PM Rating: Good

How much for one of these?

Correct stats
QuoteReply
# Mar 05 2001 at 3:29 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361204384
2 posts
I have read on another website( castersrealm) that this item adds also 5int, 5str, 5dex, 5agi, 5cha as well as the ones listed above. Can someone who actually has one of these rings plz tell me the corrrect stats? thanks much :)
▲ Page top
RE: Correct stats
QuoteReply
# May 13 2003 at 5:21 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
You're likely confusing this with the diamond wedding band from TOFS, which I believe has those stats (either that, or Castersrealm did).

The correct stats for the imbued platinum diamond ring are 5 wis, 5 sta, 5sv all resists. Wt 0.1, all races, all classes, Mithaniel Marr worshippers only.
▲ Page top
RE: Correct stats
QuoteReply
# Mar 20 2001 at 1:21 PM Rating: Default

Anonymous

I will assume the screenshot above to be more accurate that the listing in the database of casters.

The stats on the picture are correct. I have one of these rings, it's awesome.

QuoteReply
# May 13 2003 at 5:29 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
Diamonds are random drops off of 50+ mobs. The combine is never trivial.

QuoteReply
# Feb 07 2001 at 2:01 PM Rating: Default

View User Forum
Anonymous
This is a very fine item.. Too bady most of the other diety specific items stink so bad, including the one's I can use (Tunare.)
▲ Page top
RE: Hmmm
QuoteReply
# May 13 2003 at 5:37 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
Well you're right partially, imbued emerald jewelry is junk.

However some of the other deities have nice imbued jewelry; imbued black pearl, imbued ivory, imbued fire opal, imbued sapphire, imbued black sapphire, and of course, imbued diamond, are preety nice compared to the unimbued versions of the same jewelry (well no such thing as unimbued ivory jewelry, but you know what I mean).

sorry to burst your bubble but solusek ro wizards have a nice ring +4ac -8str +8int +10hp +45mp +4svf (though i think it should be more svf)

some may complain there is -8str well as a wizard i dont really need str but having two rings with 8int each and 45mp that certainly adds quite a bit to mana pool not all diety jewelry sucks

RE: Hmmm
QuoteReply
# Feb 07 2001 at 5:36 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591752464385
46 posts
So far, this is the only diety specific jewelry that I would use, with the possible exception of Ruby. It's kind of odd how they make the religeous jewelry harder to get/make and then make it so much worse then the normal stuff.

Since it requires 2 sources of enchantment, you would think that they would make it better then something that requires only 1 source of enchantment.
▲ Page top
This is a great ring
QuoteReply
# Feb 08 2001 at 1:16 PM Rating: Good
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I was shocked when I saw the stats, sure I lose 2 to the resists, but that 5 wisdom and 5 stamina is great.

It compliments a Golem Tear Ring nicely, or for raids, lets me take that off and put on a Plat Jascinth ring and keep the WIS up....

For Paladins and Clerics this is a great ring.
